generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleSpeedController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller (Vedder) converting desired speed commands into motor voltage for speed control of the autonomous vehicle chassis"
]

instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Embedded steering servo system managing real-time steering angle adjustments for the autonomous vehicle chassis"
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "ACC control logic executed off-board (on Digital Twin Entity) computing desired speed based on lead vehicle distance (ISO 15622, Performance Class I)"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR generating 3D environmental perception point clouds for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance DepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera providing supplementary 3D spatial mapping"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensors measuring real-time ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle) from chassis components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Data Transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance DepthCameraPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DepthCameraPointCloudData
]

instance ProjectedPathData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ObjectTrackData
]

// C5: Data Models
instance ObjectTrackDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "SQL database storing detected object tracks derived from LiDAR/depth camera point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
]

instance VehicleStateDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "SQL database storing ego vehicle state data (speed, steering angle) for historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
]

instance GeneratedReports : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "SQL database storing performance reports generated by Reporting Feature Engine"
]

// C6: Simulation Models
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink model implementing DBSCAN algorithm for point cloud clustering from LiDAR/depth camera data"
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink model implementing Kalman filter for object tracking from clustered point clouds"
]

instance PathProjectionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink model performing sensor fusion and path projection from tracked objects"
]

instance SensorCalibrationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Sensor calibration models ensuring accurate LiDAR/depth camera data processing"
]

instance ACCControlAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO 15622 compliant ACC control algorithm (Performance Class I) executed off-board"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2NodeEnabler
]

// C7: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) middleware enabling inter-entity communication between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationService
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database infrastructure for persistent storage of vehicle state data, object tracks, and generated reports"
    DTDFVocab:enables ReportingService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment for simulation models (point cloud processing, tracking, path projection)"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationValidationService
]

instance ROS2NodeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 node lifecycle management for deploying ACC control logic and feature engines"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonHardware
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Translation Feature Engine handling serialization of sensor data aligned with ISO 23247 standards"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D Visualization Feature Engine leveraging ROS2's RViz for interactive rendering of ego vehicle and environment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonHardware
]

// C8: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization service providing 3D visualization of ego vehicle speed, steering angle, detected object tracks, and projected driving path"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PathProjectionInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Off-board ACC control logic execution service relieving resource constraints on vehicle ECUs"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User-controlled operations service enabling ACC enable/disable, speed setting, and emergency braking via Control Panel Feature Engine"
    DTDFVocab:provides UserControlAction
]

instance DataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Feature engine collecting and processing sensor data for Digital Twin Entity"
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackingData
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateData
]

instance ACCControllingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "ACC control logic service computing desired speed based on lead vehicle distance (ISO 15622, Performance Class I)"
    DTDFVocab:provides SpeedCommandAction
]

instance ReportingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating historical performance reports from stored data"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceAnalyticsInsight
]

instance SimulationValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-driven validation service validating ACC control logic via MATLAB/Simulink models"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicValidationInsight
]

// C10: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization of ego vehicle state and environment for monitoring purposes"
]

instance PathProjectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected driving path visualization based on sensor fusion and object tracking"
]

instance ACCControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic ACC control action executing off-board speed commands based on lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance UserControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual user control action enabling/disabling ACC or setting desired speed"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ObjectTrackingData : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data action providing detected object tracks for path planning"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data action providing real-time ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle) for control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Speed command action transmitted from Digital Twin Entity to physical vehicle for actuation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical performance analytics including predictive maintenance insights (e.g., motor health monitoring)"
]

instance ControlLogicValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation-driven validation insights for ACC control logic accuracy"
]

// C11: Hardware Platforms
instance NVIDIAJetsonHardware : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson NX/Orin Nano hardware platform hosting compute-intensive tasks (ACC control, sensor processing)"
]

instance TraxxasVehicleChassis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle chassis (retrofitted from teleoperated Traxxas vehicle) with embedded lower-level steering/speed control components"
]

instance HokuyoLiDARHardware : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor hardware providing 3D environmental perception"
]

instance IntelDepthCameraHardware : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera hardware for supplementary 3D spatial mapping"
]

// C12: Environment
instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains NVIDIAJetsonHardware
    base:contains ROS2Middleware
    base:contains SQLDatabaseEnabler
    base:contains MATLABSimulinkEnabler
    base:contains DataTranslationEnabler
    base:contains VisualizationEnabler
    base:contains DataMonitoringService
    base:contains ComputationOffloadingService
    base:contains RemoteManagementService
]

instance PhysicalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TraxxasVehicleChassis
    base:contains HokuyoLiDARHardware
    base:contains IntelDepthCameraHardware
    base:contains VehicleStateSensor
    base:contains VehicleSpeedController
    base:contains SteeringServoActuator
    base:contains AdaptiveCruiseControlController
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The virtual-to-physical interaction occurs through the Digital Twin Entity’s Controlling FE, which computes ACC commands (desired speed) based on lead vehicle distance. These commands are transmitted back to the PT via Wi-Fi network (UDP-based) to actuate the Motor Actuation FE and Steering Servo Actuation FE. Emergency braking commands can also be sent remotely via the Control Panel User Interface FE, ensuring real-time synchronization with ≤100 ms latency."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates at a **real-time time-scale**, with synchronization requirements aligned to the physical twin’s operational cadence. Key time-scale specifications include: (1) **Data Collection Rate**: Object tracks and ego vehicle state updated every perception cycle (typically <100 ms); (2) **Latency Constraint**: End-to-end latency between vehicle and DT Entity ≤100 ms (3GPP TS 22.185); (3) **Clock Synchronization**: Devices synchronized to within 100 ms accuracy for event ordering and data integrity (due to hardware limitations)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation consists of three primary entities (ISO 23247 framework) with custom adaptations: (1) **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensor processing (LiDAR/Depth Camera), actuation (motor/steering), and data pre-processing FE; (2) **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages core DT functions (data collection, visualization, ACC control), split into real-time (ACC Controlling FE) and offline (Reporting FE) components; (3) **User Entity**: Provides interfaces via 3D Visualization FE (for monitoring) and Control Panel FE (for remote management). Data Translation FE ensures inter-entity communication compatibility."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin spans the following lifecycle stages: (1) **Design Phase**: Used as a cyber-physical experimentation platform for ACC system validation (combined with 3D CAD/Simulink for simulation); (2) **Operational Phase**: Enables real-time monitoring, remote management, and predictive maintenance (e.g., health monitoring of motors via DT data); (3) **Lifecycle Integration**: Supports digital threads for product lifecycle management (end-to-end information flow from design to operation)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through: (1) **Verification**: Real-time latency testing (3GPP TS 22.185), clock synchronization (±100 ms), and ISO 15622 ACC compliance; (2) **Validation**: Cross-validation with physical twin’s sensor data (LiDAR/Depth Camera), DBSCAN + Kalman filtering for object tracking accuracy; (3) **Uncertainty Management**: Data Translation FE handles unit conversions and message alignment to mitigate communication errors; (4) **Safety**: Human-in-the-loop (Fully Autonomous Digital Twin, Liberty to act vs. Ability to act taxonomy) ensures operator oversight during remote management."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical connections include: (1) **Communication Protocols**: ROS2 (for intra-entity communication) and Wi-Fi (UDP-based, for inter-entity communication); (2) **Network Architecture**: Point-to-point connections between vehicle and DT Entity with ≤100 ms end-to-end latency; (3) **Data Serialization**: Data Translation FE standardizes message formats (e.g., OPC UA alignment for manufacturing-inspired adaptations); (4) **Synchronization**: NTP-like mechanisms (via ROS2 time synchronization) ensuring ±100 ms clock accuracy."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is deployed on a hybrid infrastructure: (1) **Onboard Hardware**: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano (for real-time processing) and ROS2 nodes on the vehicle; (2) **Offboard Hosting**: Data storage (SQL databases) and visualization (3D Visualization FE) hosted externally to the vehicle; (3) **Middleware**: ROS2 orchestrates component interactions across onboard/offboard systems; (4) **Scalability**: Lightweight real-time components (e.g., ACC Controlling FE) execute on-device, while offline tasks (e.g., report generation) run remotely."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration occurs with external systems via: (1) **Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) Communication**: Wi-Fi-based data exchange with other autonomous vehicles (e.g., lead vehicle tracking); (2) **Cloud Services**: Offloading compute-intensive tasks (e.g., data storage, visualization) to external cloud platforms; (3) **Standardized Protocols**: OPC UA (for data translation) and ROS2 (for inter-system messaging), enabling compatibility with other DTs or IoT systems."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is governed by the PT owner, with the DT service provider managing data processing and visualization. Privacy considerations include: (1) **Encryption**: Data in transit (Wi-Fi) is assumed to be encrypted (though not explicitly detailed in the documents); (2) **Access Controls**: User authentication via Control Panel FE restricts remote management access; (3) **Anonymization**: Object tracks and ego vehicle state are processed to avoid personal identification (e.g., no real-time driver-specific data exposure)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin adheres to the following standards: (1) **ISO 23247**: Reference architecture adapted for automotive DTs (entity-based structure, Data Translation FE); (2) **ISO 15622**: ACC control algorithm compliance (Performance Class I); (3) **3GPP TS 22.185**: Latency requirements for V2V communication (<100 ms); (4) **ROS2**: Middleware for inter-entity communication; (5) **OPC UA**: Data translation alignment for manufacturing-inspired adaptations."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through: (1) **Remote Management Safeguards**: Human-in-the-loop (Fully Autonomous Digital Twin) with operator oversight during remote operations; (2) **Latency Constraints**: ≤100 ms end-to-end latency to prevent catastrophic failures; (3) **Clock Synchronization**: ±100 ms accuracy to ensure event ordering integrity; (4) **Data Integrity**: Data Translation FE mitigates communication errors; (5) **Emergency Protocols**: Direct emergency braking command support via Control Panel FE. Human safety is prioritized over autonomous decision-making, aligning with ISO 23247’s safety-critical domain considerations."
]
